title: Citrusy Squid Stew
description: A comforting and flavorful citrusy squid stew that is both vegetarian-friendly and reminiscent of a Syrian dish, perfect for those feeling under the weather.
instructions: Clean and slice the baby squid into bite-sized pieces.
Dice the onion, finely chop the garlic, grate the zest from one citrus fruit, set aside. If using dried orange peel, crush it lightly.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add sliced onions and sauté until translucent. Stir in crushed citrus peel (if using), chopped garlic, and grated lemon/lime zest.

Add squid to the pan with vegetable broth and bay leaves. Season with salt and pepper as needed. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 15 minutes until squid is tender but still chewy.
Meanwhile, slice bread into cubes. In an oven-safe casserole or large baking dish, spread the bread cubes evenly in the bottom.
Layer the cooked squid mixture over the top of the bread cubes in the dish. Spread remaining lemon and lime zest on top. Pour the reserved broth over the top.
Bake in preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes until bubbling and topping is golden brown.
Serve hot with additional citrus wedges on the side.
